clinical_coherence.notes: 27F Hispanic with mild asthma and a previously unfollowed ovarian cyst presenting with classic NMDAR encephalitis. The prior ovarian cyst history is a clever detail linking to the likely teratoma trigger. Clinical picture is coherent. PMH is slightly less templated than S02/S03 (no PCOS), but the overall clinical phenotype is very similar.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF shows lymphocytic pleocytosis (75 cells), elevated protein (62.3), normal glucose (64.7), positive NMDAR antibodies at 1:256. EEG with extreme delta brush. All concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G04.81 correct. The prior unfollowed ovarian cyst linking to the teratoma trigger is a good teaching point about missed diagnoses.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 27F Hispanic is realistic. The prior unfollowed ovarian cyst is a realistic clinical detail.

educational_value.notes: The prior unfollowed ovarian cyst linking to the teratoma trigger adds a unique teaching point about missed diagnoses and the importance of follow-up. This differentiates it somewhat from S01-S03.

issues_found: Fourth straightforward NMDAR case with very similar clinical phenotype and CSF profiles
strengths: Prior unfollowed ovarian cyst is a clever link to the teratoma trigger
Teaches about the importance of follow-up on incidental findings
